steps.title: Preparation of Carrier RNA (1 μg/μL)
steps.instruction: Preparation of Carrier RNA (1 μg/μL)

steps.title: Add Buffer AVE to the tube...
steps.instruction: Add Buffer AVE to the tube containing Lyophilized Carrier RNA to obtain a solution of 1 μg/μl (i.e., add 310 μl Buffer AVE to 310 μg lyophilized carrier RNA, or 1550 μl Buffer AVE to 1550 μg lyophilized carrier RNA; check the tube label for content).

steps.title: Preparation of Buffer AVL + carrier RNA Mix
steps.instruction: Preparation of Buffer AVL + carrier RNA Mix

steps.title: Check Buffer AVL for precipitate, and...
steps.instruction: Check Buffer AVL for precipitate, and if necessary incubate at 80 °C until the precipitate is dissolved

steps.title: Calculate the volume of Buffer AVL–carrier...
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of Buffer AVL–carrier RNA mix needed per batch of samples by selecting the number of samples to be simultaneously processed from Table below . Buffer AVL–carrier RNA should be prepared fresh, and is stable at 2–8°C for up to 48 h. This solution develops a precipitate when stored at 2–8°C that must be dissolved by warming at 80°C before use. Do not warm Buffer AVL–carrier RNA solution more than 6 times. Do not incubate at 80°C for more than 5 minutes. Frequent warming and extended incubation will cause degradation of carrier RNA For larger numbers of samples, volumes can be calculated using the following sample calculation: n*0.56ml=y ml y ml*10 (µl/ml)=z µl Where; n = number of samples to be processed simultaneously y = calculated volume of Buffer AVL z = volume of carrier RNA to add to Buffer AVL

steps.title: Preparation of AW1
steps.instruction: Preparation of AW1

steps.title: Before using for the first time,...
steps.instruction: Before using for the first time, add the appropriate amount of ethanol (96–100%) as indicated on the bottle of Buffer AW1

steps.title: Preparation of AW2
steps.instruction: Preparation of AW2

steps.title: Before using for the first time,...
steps.instruction: Before using for the first time, add the appropriate amount of ethanol (96–100%) as indicated on the bottle of buffer AW2

steps.title: Procedure
steps.instruction: Procedure

steps.title: Pipette 560 µL prepared Buffer AVL...
steps.instruction: Pipette 560 µL prepared Buffer AVL containing carrier RNA into a 1.5 ml Microcentrifuge tube If the sample volume is larger than 140 μl, increase the amount of Buffer AVL–carrier RNA proportionally (e.g., a 280 μl sample will require 1120 μl Buffer AVL–carrier RNA) and use a larger tube. If the sample volume is smaller than 140 μl, top up with the required volume of PBS buffer

steps.title: Add 140 µL Sample to the...
steps.instruction: Add 140 µL Sample to the Buffer AVL–carrier RNA in the Microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the tube to remove...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the tube to remove drops from the inside of the lid

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 10 min Viral particle lysis is complete after lysis for 10 min at room temperature. Longer incubation times have no effect on the yield or quality of the purified RNA

steps.title: Mix by pulse-vortexing for 15 s...
steps.instruction: Mix by pulse-vortexing for 15 s To ensure efficient lysis, it is essential that the sample is mixed thoroughly with Buffer AVL to yield a homogeneous solution. Frozen samples that have only been thawed once can also be used

steps.title: Add 560 μl ethanol (96–100%) to...
steps.instruction: Add 560 μl ethanol (96–100%) to the sample, and mix by pulse-vortexing for 15 s. After mixing, briefly centrifuge the tube to remove drops from inside the lid. Use only ethanol, since other alcohols may result in reduced RNA yield and purity. Do not use denatured alcohol, which contains other substances, such as methanol or methylethylketone. If the sample volume is greater than 140 μl, increase the amount of ethanol proportionally (e.g., a 280 μl sample will require 1120 μl ethanol). To ensure efficient binding, it is essential that the sample is mixed thoroughly with the ethanol to yield a homogeneous solution

steps.title: Carefully apply 630 μl of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully apply 630 μl of the solution from step 5 to the QIAamp Mini column (in a 2 ml collection tube) without wetting the rim.

steps.title: Close the cap, and centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Close the cap, and centrifuge at 6000 xg (8000 rpm) for 1 min.

steps.title: Place the QIAamp Mini column into...
steps.instruction: Place the QIAamp Mini column into a clean 2 ml collection tube, and discard the tube containing the filtrate. Close each spin column to avoid cross-contamination during centrifugation. Centrifugation is performed at 6000 xg (8000 rpm) to limit Microcentrifuge noise. Centrifugation at full speed will not affect the yield or purity of the viral RNA. If the solution has not completely passed through the membrane, centrifuge again at a higher speed until all of the solution has passed through

steps.title: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and repeat step 6
steps.instruction: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and repeat step 6. If the sample volume was greater than 140 μl, repeat this step until all of the lysate has been loaded onto the spin column

steps.title: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column,...
steps.instruction: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and add 500 μl Buffer AW1

steps.title: Close the cap, and centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Close the cap, and centrifuge at 6000 xg (8000 rpm) for 1 min

steps.title: Place the QIAamp Mini column in...
steps.instruction: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a clean 2 ml collection tube (provided), and discard the tube containing the filtrate. It is not necessary to increase the volume of Buffer AW1 even if the original sample volume was larger than 140 μl.

steps.title: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column,...
steps.instruction: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column, and add 500 μl Buffer AW2

steps.title: Close the cap and centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Close the cap and centrifuge at full speed (20,000 xg; 14,000 rpm) for 3 min Residual Buffer AW2 in the eluate may cause problems in downstream applications. Some centrifuge rotors may vibrate upon deceleration, resulting in flow-through, containing Buffer AW2, contacting the QIAamp Mini column. Removing the QIAamp Mini column and collection tube from the rotor may also cause flow-through to come into contact with the QIAamp Mini column. In consideration of these scenarios, the next step should always be performed.

steps.title: Place the QIAamp Mini column in...
steps.instruction: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a new 2 ml collection tube (not provided), and discard the old collection tube with the filtrate

steps.title: Centrifuge at full speed for 1 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at full speed for 1 min

steps.title: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a clean 1
steps.instruction: Place the QIAamp Mini column in a clean 1.5 ml Microcentrifuge tube (not provided) and discard the old collection tube containing the filtrate

steps.title: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column...
steps.instruction: Carefully open the QIAamp Mini column and add 60 μl Buffer AVE equilibrated to room temperature

steps.title: Close the cap, and incubate at...
steps.instruction: Close the cap, and incubate at room temperature for 1 min

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6000 xg (8000 rpm)...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6000 xg (8000 rpm) for 1 min A single elution with 60 μl Buffer AVE is sufficient to elute at least 90% of the viral RNA from the QIAamp Mini column. Performing a double elution using 2 x 40 μl Buffer AVE will increase yield by up to 10%. Elution with volumes of less than 30 μl will lead to reduced yields and will not increase the final concentration of RNA in the eluate

steps.title: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s
steps.instruction: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s

steps.title: Preparation of low titer samples
steps.instruction: Preparation of low titer samples

steps.title: Turn on a refrigerated centrifuge, secure...
steps.instruction: Turn on a refrigerated centrifuge, secure the rotor, close the lid and set to 4˚C, allow the centrifuge and rotor to reduce in temperature to 4˚C. This will take approximately 30 minutes 00:30:00 . If you are unsure how to transfer the rotor or use the centrifuge, ask for advice from the supervisor before attempting to use

steps.title: Transfer 1mL of the samples i
steps.instruction: Transfer 1mL of the samples i.e., plasma, serum, urine, cerebrospinal fluid, bone marrow and other body fluids that have very low viral titers to a 1.5ml microcentrifuge tube under a safety cabinet. Ensure equal volumes and tubes so that they are balanced.

steps.title: Transfer the tubes to the preset refrigerated centrifuge at 4℃
steps.instruction: Transfer the tubes to the preset refrigerated centrifuge at 4℃.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed of 45,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed of 45,000 xg for one (1) hour. Ensure the samples are well closed and balanced.

steps.title: After centrifugation, carefully disperse off the...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, carefully disperse off the top most part of the supernatant leaving a desired volume at the bottom. Ensure that the pellet is not dislodged.

steps.title: Record Reagent Lot Number
steps.instruction: Record Reagent Lot Number: Superscript IV One-Step RT-PCR System Thermo Scientific Catalog #12594100

steps.title: Preparation of One-Step RT-PCR
steps.instruction: Preparation of One-Step RT-PCR

steps.title: Thaw aliquoted 2x RT-PCR Master Mix...
steps.instruction: Thaw aliquoted 2x RT-PCR Master Mix Buffer, and Primers at room temperature. Vortex briefly and keep on ice until use.

steps.title: Invert Superscript IV RT Mix to mix before use
steps.instruction: Invert Superscript IV RT Mix to mix before use. Keep on ice.

steps.title: Save the following SIV RT-PCR program...
steps.instruction: Save the following SIV RT-PCR program on the thermal cycler; Choose the preheat lid option (105°C) Set the reaction volume to 13 μl 50 °C for 10 minutes 98 °C for 2 minutes 40 cycles of: 98°C for 10 seconds 62°C for 10 seconds 72°C for 45 seconds* 72°C for 5 minutes Hold at 4°C “*” Extension Time varies with the set of primers; adjust for 30 sec/kb.

steps.title: Record the; Target Amplicon Size (bp) Extension Time
steps.instruction: Record the; Target Amplicon Size (bp) Extension Time

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 ml tube, combine the following volumes to prepare the RT-PCR Reaction Mix . Multiply each volume by the number of reactions including a negative and positive control. A 10% reagent overage is included to account for small pipetting errors; A B C 1 Reagents Volume (μl) ………….. x No. of Reactions 2 Nuclease-free Water 2.3375 3 2x RT-PCR Master Mix Buffer 6.875 4 Forward primer (25 µM) 0.275 5 Reverse primer (25 μM) 0.275 6 Superscript IV RT Mix 0.1375 The table below details the primers used to amplify the protease and reverse transcriptase genes as one amplicon, and the integrase gene as a separate amplicon. These amplicons are then combined for downstream library preparation. These primers are modified based on the most circulating HIV-1 subtypes A and D in Uganda. For re-use, refer to the HXB2 location in the table below; %s %s Primer map

steps.title: Mix gently and ensure all the...
steps.instruction: Mix gently and ensure all the components are at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly if needed and keep on ice
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly if needed and keep on ice. For choice of primers, refer to the table below;

steps.title: Label sterile 0
steps.instruction: Label sterile 0.2 mL 8-tube strips with sample details. Put on a frozen ice-rack.

steps.title: Add 9 μl of the RT-PCR...
steps.instruction: Add 9 μl of the RT-PCR reaction mix to each of the labelled tubes

steps.title: Cover tubes with strip caps and...
steps.instruction: Cover tubes with strip caps and transfer to the sample preparation room

steps.title: In the sample preparation room, add 3
steps.instruction: In the sample preparation room, add 3.5 μl of RNA samples to the corresponding wells. Include a positive control from the previous batches, and nuclease-free water as a negative control. Record samples added.

steps.title: Mix by pipetting up and down...
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times, and centrifuge briefly

steps.title: Transfer to the PCR room, place...
steps.instruction: Transfer to the PCR room, place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler and run the RT-PCR program

steps.title: Remove from the thermal cycler, centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Remove from the thermal cycler, centrifuge briefly, and proceed with the visualisation Refer to Gel electrophoresis and visualization steps described in the next Section. Other approaches could be used.

steps.title: Store RT-PCR amplicons at -20°C for future use
steps.instruction: Store RT-PCR amplicons at -20°C for future use.

steps.title: Record sample preparation details in the...
steps.instruction: Record sample preparation details in the table below; A B C D E F G 1 Tube No. Lab ID Sample Type (RNA, cDNA) RT-PCR Forward Primer RT-PCR Reverse Primers RT-PCR Results (Amplified or Failed) 2 3 4 5

steps.title: Record the reagent lot numbers
steps.instruction: Record the reagent lot numbers: Ultrapure Agarose Thermo Scientific Catalog #16500500 GeneRuler 1 kb DNA Ladder Thermo Scientific Catalog #SM0312 DNA Gel Loading Dye (6X) Thermo Scientific Catalog #R0611 SYBR™ Safe DNA Gel Stain Thermo Scientific Catalog #S33102 UltraPure™ DNA Typing Grade™ 50X TAE Buffer Thermo Scientific Catalog #24710030

steps.title: Preparation of an agarose gel electrophoresis apparatus
steps.instruction: Preparation of an agarose gel electrophoresis apparatus

steps.title: Determine the amount of agarose solution...
steps.instruction: Determine the amount of agarose solution needed to cast your gel. Refer to the table below: A B C 1 Fragment Size % Agarose (in 1X TAE) % Prepared 2 1000 to 23,000 0.6 □ 3 800 to 10,000 0.8 □ 4 400 to 8,000 1 □ 5 300 to 7,000 1.2 □ 6 200 to 4,000 1.5 □ 7 100 to 3,000 2 □ Remember to take the thickness of the gel into account, as it affects both well volume and power requirements

steps.title: Prepare an agarose gel by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare an agarose gel by adding agarose powder to 1x TAE buffer i.e., for a 1% agarose, add 1 g ofagarose powder to 100 ml TAE 1x buffer in a conical flask.

steps.title: Record the; Amount of Agarose powder...
steps.instruction: Record the; Amount of Agarose powder Volume of 1x TAE buffer

steps.title: Mix the ingredients and dissolve the...
steps.instruction: Mix the ingredients and dissolve the agarose in the microwave by heating the mixture just to the boiling point. Approximately, 1.5 minutes.

steps.title: Remove the conical flask with a...
steps.instruction: Remove the conical flask with a heavy-duty rubber glove and cool off the agarose mixture to hand temperature before adding Sybr Safe DNA Gel Stain.

steps.title: Add 10 µl of Sybr Safe...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µl of Sybr Safe DNA Gel Stain to the molten agarose, mix gently by swirling the conical flask until homogenous.

steps.title: Assemble gel casting system by placing...
steps.instruction: Assemble gel casting system by placing the gel tray in the casting holder and placing the combs in the gel tray, and pour in the agarose mixture.

steps.title: When the agarose gel has solidified,...
steps.instruction: When the agarose gel has solidified, prepare the parafilm and dispense 2µl drops of 6X DNA gel loading dye solution onto parafilm for the number of samples to load, or pipette 2µl of 6X into 8-well strips for bulk loading.

steps.title: Add 2 µl of each amplified...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µl of each amplified PCR product and mix by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Pipette the mixture and dispense it...
steps.instruction: Pipette the mixture and dispense it into the appropriate well of the gel. Be sure to include the positive and negative control on your gel.

steps.title: Briefly vortex the Gene Ruler 1...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex the Gene Ruler 1 kb DNA Ladder and dispense 2µl in the dedicated slots.

steps.title: Place the agarose gel in the...
steps.instruction: Place the agarose gel in the horizontal electrophoresis system and run for about 30 minutes at 100 Volts.

steps.title: After the gel electrophoresis is completed,...
steps.instruction: After the gel electrophoresis is completed, place the gel on the gel imaging system i.e., iBright Imaging System

steps.title: A Nested PCR is an additional...
steps.instruction: A Nested PCR is an additional step to troubleshoot the samples that have failed to amplify by the RT-PCR Record the reagent lot numbers: Platinum SuperFi II PCR Master Mix (2X) Thermo Scientific Catalog #12368050

steps.title: Preparation of PCR amplification
steps.instruction: Preparation of PCR amplification

steps.title: Thaw primers at room temperature
steps.instruction: Thaw primers at room temperature. Vortex briefly and keep on ice until use. The choice of the nested primers should be based on the external primers that were in the table above. Refer to the Primer map

steps.title: Invert Platinum SuperFi II Master Mix to mix before use
steps.instruction: Invert Platinum SuperFi II Master Mix to mix before use. Keep on ice.

steps.title: Save the following SuperFi PCR program...
steps.instruction: Save the following SuperFi PCR program on the thermal cycler; Choose the preheat lid option (105°C) Set the reaction volume to 13 μl 98°C for 30 Seconds 35 cycles of: 98°C for 10 seconds 60°C for 10 seconds 72°C for 45 seconds* 72°C for 5 minutes Hold at 4°C “*” Extension Time varies with primer sets; adjust for 30 sec/kb.

steps.title: Record the; Target Amplicon Size (bp) Extension Time
steps.instruction: Record the; Target Amplicon Size (bp) Extension Time

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 ml tube, combine the following volumes to prepare the PCR Reaction Mix based on choice of the enzyme. Multiply each volume by the number of reactions including a negative and positive control. A 10% reagent overage is included to account for small pipetting errors; A B C 1 Reagents Volume (μl) ………….. x No. of Reactions 2 Nuclease free water 5.225 3 2X SuperFi II Master Mix 6.875 4 Forward Primer (25 pmol/μl) 0.275 5 Reverse Primer (25 pmol/μl) 0.275 SuperFi II DNA polymerase PCR Reaction Mix The choice of Nested primers should be based on the External primers used in the RT-PCR step. Refer to the primer map above.

steps.title: Label sterile 0
steps.instruction: Label sterile 0.2 mL 8-tube strips with sample details. Put on a frozen ice-rack.

steps.title: Add 11
steps.instruction: Add 11.5 μl of the PCR reaction mix to each of the labelled tubes

steps.title: Cover tubes with strip caps and...
steps.instruction: Cover tubes with strip caps and transfer them to the PCR room

steps.title: In the PCR room, add 1...
steps.instruction: In the PCR room, add 1 μl of target DNA or cDNA samples to the corresponding wells. Include a positive control from the previous batches, and nuclease-free water as a negative control. Record samples added

steps.title: Mix by pipetting up and down...
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times, and centrifuge briefly

steps.title: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler and run the PCR program

steps.title: Remove from the thermal cycler, centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Remove from the thermal cycler, centrifuge briefly and proceed with the visualisation Refer to Gel electrophoresis and visualization steps described in the above Section. Other approaches could be used.

steps.title: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s
steps.instruction: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s

steps.title: Store RT-PCR amplicons at -20°C for future use
steps.instruction: Store RT-PCR amplicons at -20°C for future use.

steps.title: Library preparation can be achieved using...
steps.instruction: Library preparation can be achieved using either Illumina or Oxford Nanopore Technology platforms. Both methods have yielded comparable results in our experiments, however additional laboratory validation can help determine the optimal approach for specific needs.

steps.title: Record the reagent lot numbers
steps.instruction: Record the reagent lot numbers: Illumina DNA Prep, (M) Tagmentation (96 Samples, IPB) Illumina, Inc. Catalog #20060059 Illumina® DNA/RNA UD Indexes Set A, B, C, D Tagmentation (96 Indexes, 96 Samples) Illumina, Inc. Catalog #20091654, 20091656, 20091658, 20

steps.title: Record Reagent lot numbers; Rapid Barcoding...
steps.instruction: Record Reagent lot numbers; Rapid Barcoding Kit 96 V14 Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-RBK114.96 AMPure XP Reagent, 450 mL Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63882

steps.title: Tagment DNA
steps.instruction: Tagment DNA

steps.title: Bring BLT and TB1 to room temperature
steps.instruction: Bring BLT and TB1 to room temperature. Vortex to mix

steps.title: Save the following TAG program on the thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Save the following TAG program on the thermal cycler: Choose the preheat lid option and set to 100°C Set the react ion volume to 50 μl 55°C for 15 minutes Hold at 10°C

steps.title: Label a new 96-well PCR plate TAG PCR
steps.instruction: Label a new 96-well PCR plate TAG PCR

steps.title: Add 2–30 μl DNA to each...
steps.instruction: Add 2–30 μl DNA to each well of TAG PCR plate so that the total input amount is 100–500 ng. If DNA volume < 30 μl, add nuclease- free water to the DNA samples to bring the total volume to 30 μl

steps.title: Vortex BLT (yellow cap) vigorously for 10 seconds to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex BLT (yellow cap) vigorously for 10 seconds to resuspend. Repeat as necessary

steps.title: Combine the following volumes to prepare the Tagmentation Master Mix
steps.instruction: Combine the following volumes to prepare the Tagmentation Master Mix . Multiply each volume by the number of samples being processed. Reagent overage is already included for pipetting errors: %s %s

steps.title: Vortex the Tagmentation Master Mix thoroughly to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex the Tagmentation Master Mix thoroughly to resuspend

steps.title: Divide the Tagmentation Master Mix volume...
steps.instruction: Divide the Tagmentation Master Mix volume equally into an 8-tube strip

steps.title: Using a 200 μl multichannel pipette,...
steps.instruction: Using a 200 μl multichannel pipette, transfer 20 μl Tagmentation Master Mix from the 8-tube strip to each well of the plate containing a sample. Use fresh tips for each sample column

steps.title: Discard the 8-tube strip after the...
steps.instruction: Discard the 8-tube strip after the Tagmentation Master Mix has been dispensed

steps.title: Pipette each sample 10 times to resuspend
steps.instruction: Pipette each sample 10 times to resuspend

steps.title: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler and run the TAG program

steps.title: Post Tagmentation Cleanup
steps.instruction: Post Tagmentation Cleanup

steps.title: Bring TSB and TWB to room temperature
steps.instruction: Bring TSB and TWB to room temperature. If precipitates are observed in TSB, heat at 37°C for 10 minutes, and then vortex until precipitates are dissolved

steps.title: Save the following PTC program on the thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Save the following PTC program on the thermal cycler: Choose the preheat lid opt ion and set to 100°C Set the react ion volume to 60 μl 37°C for 15 minutes Hold at 10°C

steps.title: Add 10 μl TSB to the plate
steps.instruction: Add 10 μl TSB to the plate

steps.title: Slowly pipette each well 10 times...
steps.instruction: Slowly pipette each well 10 times to resuspend the beads, and then seal

steps.title: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler and run the PTC program

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (~3 minutes)

steps.title: Using a multichannel pipette, remove and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Using a multichannel pipette, remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash two times as follows
steps.instruction: Wash two times as follows: Remove the sample plate from the magnetic stand and use a deliberately slow pipetting technique to add 100 μl TWB directly onto the beads. A deliberately slow pipetting technique minimizes the potential of TWB foaming to avoid incorrect volume aspiration and incomplete mixing. Pipette slowly until beads are fully resuspended. Place the plate on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~3 minutes). Using a multichannel pipette, remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Remove the plate from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the magnetic stand and use a deliberately slow pipetting technique to add 100 μl TWB directly onto the beads

steps.title: Pipette each well slowly to resuspend the beads
steps.instruction: Pipette each well slowly to resuspend the beads

steps.title: Seal the plate and place on...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate and place on the magnetic stand until the liquid is clear (~3 minutes) Keep on the magnetic stand and proceed to Amplify Tagmented DNA step. The TWB remains in the wells to prevent over drying of the beads

steps.title: Amplify Tagmented DNA This step amplifies...
steps.instruction: Amplify Tagmented DNA This step amplifies the tagmented DNA using a limited-cycle PCR program. The PCR step adds Index 1 (i7) adapters, Index 2 (i5) adapters, and sequences required for sequencing cluster generation

steps.title: Thaw EPM on ice
steps.instruction: Thaw EPM on ice. Invert to mix, then briefly centrifuge

steps.title: Thaw index adapters at room temperature
steps.instruction: Thaw index adapters at room temperature. If tubes; vortex to mix, then centrifuge briefly. If plate; spin briefly before use

steps.title: Save the following BLT PCR program...
steps.instruction: Save the following BLT PCR program on a thermal cycler using the appropriate number of PCR cycles. Choose the preheat lid option and set to 100°C 68°C for 3 minutes 98°C for 3 minutes (*) cycles of: 98°C for 45 seconds 62°C for 30 seconds 68°C for 2 minutes 68°C for 1minutes Hold at 10°C "*" Choose the number of cycles in reference to the below; %s %s

steps.title: Record the cycles used for each sample
steps.instruction: Record the cycles used for each sample

steps.title: Combine the following volumes to prepare the PCR Master Mix
steps.instruction: Combine the following volumes to prepare the PCR Master Mix. Multiply each volume by the number of samples being processed. Reagent overage is included in the volume to ensure accurate pipetting; %s %s

steps.title: Vortex, and then centrifuge the PCR...
steps.instruction: Vortex, and then centrifuge the PCR Master Mix at 280 × g for 10 seconds

steps.title: With the TAG PCR plate on...
steps.instruction: With the TAG PCR plate on the magnetic stand, use a 200 μl multichannel pipette to remove and discard supernatant. Foam that remains on the well walls does not adversely affect the library

steps.title: Remove from the magnet, and immediately...
steps.instruction: Remove from the magnet, and immediately add 40 μl PCR Master Mix directly onto the beads in each sample well

steps.title: Immediately pipette to mix until the beads are fully resuspended
steps.instruction: Immediately pipette to mix until the beads are fully resuspended. Alternatively, seal the plate and use a plate shaker at 1600 rpm for 1 minute

steps.title: Seal the sample plate and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Seal the sample plate and centrifuge at 280 × g for 3 seconds

steps.title: Add the appropriate index adapters to each sample
steps.instruction: Add the appropriate index adapters to each sample

steps.title: Using a pipette set to 40...
steps.instruction: Using a pipette set to 40 μl, pipette 10 times to mix. Alternatively, seal the plate and use a plate shaker at 1600 rpm for 1 minute

steps.title: Seal the plate with foil, and...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with foil, and then centrifuge at 280 × g for 30 seconds

steps.title: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler and run the BLT PCR program Safe Stopping Point

steps.title: If you are stopping; Record the...
steps.instruction: If you are stopping; Record the Stop Time Store at 2°C to 8°C for up to 30 days

steps.title: Clean Up Libraries This step uses...
steps.instruction: Clean Up Libraries This step uses double-sided bead purification procedure to purify the amplified libraries.

steps.title: Thaw IPB to room temperature
steps.instruction: Thaw IPB to room temperature. Vortex and invert to mix

steps.title: Thaw RSB and bring to room temperature
steps.instruction: Thaw RSB and bring to room temperature. Vortex to mix

steps.title: Prepare fresh 80% Ethanol enough for all samples
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 80% Ethanol enough for all samples

steps.title: Centrifuge sample plate at 280 ×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge sample plate at 280 × g for 1 minute to collect contents at the bot tom of the well

steps.title: Place the TAG PCR plate on...
steps.instruction: Place the TAG PCR plate on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~5 minutes)

steps.title: Label a new 96 well PCR plate Wash1
steps.instruction: Label a new 96 well PCR plate Wash1

steps.title: Transfer 45 μl supernatant from each...
steps.instruction: Transfer 45 μl supernatant from each well of the TAG PCR plate to the corresponding well of Wash1

steps.title: Vortex and invert IPB multiple times to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex and invert IPB multiple times to resuspend

steps.title: Add 40 μl nuclease-free water to...
steps.instruction: Add 40 μl nuclease-free water to each well of Wash1 containing supernatant

steps.title: Add 45 μl IPB to each well containing supernatant
steps.instruction: Add 45 μl IPB to each well containing supernatant

steps.title: Pipette each well 10 times to mix
steps.instruction: Pipette each well 10 times to mix. Alternatively, seal the plate and use a plate shaker at 1600 rpm for 1 minute

steps.title: Seal the plate and incubate at...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate and incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Place on the magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Place on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~5 minutes)

steps.title: During incubation, thoroughly vortex the IPB...
steps.instruction: During incubation, thoroughly vortex the IPB (undiluted stock tube), and label a new 96-well PCR plate Wash2

steps.title: Add 15 μl to each well of Wash2 plate
steps.instruction: Add 15 μl to each well of Wash2 plate

steps.title: Transfer 125 μl supernatant from each...
steps.instruction: Transfer 125 μl supernatant from each well of the Wash1 plate into the corresponding well of the Wash2 containing 15 μl undiluted IPB

steps.title: Pipette each well 10 times to mix
steps.instruction: Pipette each well 10 times to mix. Alternatively, seal the plate and use a plate shaker at 1600 rpm for 1 minute

steps.title: Discard Wash1
steps.instruction: Discard Wash1

steps.title: Incubate Wash2 plate at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate Wash2 plate at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Place on the magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Place on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~5 minutes)

steps.title: Without disturbing the beads, remove and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Without disturbing the beads, remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Proceed to wash with 80% Ethanol
steps.instruction: Proceed to wash with 80% Ethanol

steps.title: Wash two times as follows; With...
steps.instruction: Wash two times as follows; With the plate on the magnetic stand, add 200 μl fresh 80% Ethanol without mixing. Incubate for 30 seconds. Without disturbing the beads, remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Use a 20 μl pipette to...
steps.instruction: Use a 20 μl pipette to remove and discard residual Ethanol

steps.title: Air-dry on the magnetic stand for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Air-dry on the magnetic stand for 5 minutes

steps.title: Remove from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove from the magnetic stand

steps.title: Add 32 μl RSB to the beads
steps.instruction: Add 32 μl RSB to the beads

steps.title: Pipette to resuspend
steps.instruction: Pipette to resuspend

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~2 minutes)

steps.title: Label new 96-well PCR plate CLEAN LIB
steps.instruction: Label new 96-well PCR plate CLEAN LIB

steps.title: Transfer 30 μl supernatant to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer 30 μl supernatant to the CLEAN LIB plate Safe Stopping Point

steps.title: If you are stopping; Record the...
steps.instruction: If you are stopping; Record the stop time Seal the plate with adhesive foil seal, and store at -25°C to -15°C for up to 30 days

steps.title: Pool Libraries This combines all libraries into one pool
steps.instruction: Pool Libraries This combines all libraries into one pool. When the DNA input is 100-500 ng, quantifying and normalizing individual libraries generated in the same experiment is unnecessary. However, the final yield of libraries generated in separate experiments can vary slightly. To achieve optimal cluster density, pool equal library volumesand quantify the pool before sequencing

steps.title: For DNA Inputs of < 100...
steps.instruction: For DNA Inputs of < 100 ng Quantify each library individually using Qubit™ dsDNA Quantification HS Assay Kits Thermo Scientific Catalog #Q32854

steps.title: For DNA Inputs of 100–500 ng...
steps.instruction: For DNA Inputs of 100–500 ng Combine 5 μl of each library (up to 384 libraries) in a 1.7 ml microcentrifuge tube. Vortex to mix, and then centrifuge. Quantify the library pool

steps.title: Check Library Quality This step assesses...
steps.instruction: Check Library Quality This step assesses quality of 1μl library or pooled libraries

steps.title: Analyze 1μl library or pooled libraries...
steps.instruction: Analyze 1μl library or pooled libraries using a Bioanalyzer or Tape station Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity DNA Analysis kit Agilent Technologies Catalog #5067-4626 High Sensitivity D1000 DNA Screen Tape assays Kits Agilent Technologies Catalog #5067-5585, 5067-5584

steps.title: Dilute Libraries to the starting concentration...
steps.instruction: Dilute Libraries to the starting concentration This step dilutes libraries to the starting concentration for the sequencing system; Miseq (4nM) Iseq (2nM)

steps.title: Calculate the molarity value of the...
steps.instruction: Calculate the molarity value of the library or pooled libraries using the following formula; ((Concentration (ng/ul))/(660(g/mol)*Average library size (bp) ))*(10^6) =Concentration (nM)

steps.title: Using the molarity value, calculate the...
steps.instruction: Using the molarity value, calculate the volumes of RSB and library needed to dilute libraries to the starting concentration for the Miseq system; Libraries quantified individually; dilute each library to the starting concentration. For multiplexed library pool: Add 10 μl of each diluted library to a tube Ensure that multiplexed libraries have unique index adapters

steps.title: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s
steps.instruction: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s

steps.title: Perform a PCR clean-up of amplicon...
steps.instruction: Perform a PCR clean-up of amplicon DNA samples using Agencourt AMPure XP beads This step ensures the removal of proteins, salts, dNTPs, and primers, which could potentially impact the library preparation and subsequent analysis workflow.

steps.title: Determine whether or not a plate...
steps.instruction: Determine whether or not a plate transfer is necessary If the PCR reaction volume multiplied by 2.8 exceeds the volume of the PCR plate, a transfer to a 300 μL round bottom plate or a 1.2 mL deep-well plate is required.

steps.title: Shake the Agencourt AMPure XP bottle...
steps.instruction: Shake the Agencourt AMPure XP bottle to resuspend any magnetic particles that may have settled. Then add Agencourt AMPure XP according to the sample reaction volume in the Table below; %s %s The volume of Agencourt AMPure XP for a given reaction can be derived from the following equation: (Volume of Agencourt AMPure XP per reaction) = 1.8 x (Reaction Volume)

steps.title: Mix reagent and sample thoroughly by pipette mixing 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix reagent and sample thoroughly by pipette mixing 10 times. Let the mixed samples incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature for maximum recovery This step binds DNA fragments 100 bp and larger to the magnetic beads. Pipette mixing is preferable to vortexing as it tends to be more reproducible. The color of the mixture should appear homogenous after mixing

steps.title: Place the reaction plate onto a...
steps.instruction: Place the reaction plate onto a 96 magnet block for 2 minutes to separate beads from the solution Wait for the solution to clear before proceeding to the next step

steps.title: Aspirate the cleared solution from the reaction plate and discard
steps.instruction: Aspirate the cleared solution from the reaction plate and discard. Leave 5 μL of supernatant behind, otherwise beads are drawn out with the supernatant. This step must be performed while the reaction plate is situated on the 96 magnet block

steps.title: While the reaction plate is on...
steps.instruction: While the reaction plate is on the 96 magnet block, dispense 200 μL of 70% ethanol to each well of the reaction plate and incubate for 30 seconds at room temperature. Aspirate out the ethanol and discard. If the total volume of sample plus reagent exceeds 200 μl, then use a wash volume of at least the volume of sample plus reagent.

steps.title: Repeat steps 17
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 17.5 and 17.6 The beads are not drawn out easily when in alcohol, so it is not necessary to leave any supernatant behind

steps.title: Air dry the beads while the...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads while the reaction plate is on the magnet block to ensure all traces of Ethanol are removed Do not over dry the bead ring (bead ring appears cracked if over dried) as this will significantly decrease elution efficiency.

steps.title: Remove the reaction plate from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the reaction plate from the magnet plate, and then add 40 μL of elution buffer to each well of the reaction plate and pipette mix 10 times. Incubate for 2 minutes. The liquid level will be high enough to contact the magnetic beads at a 40 μL elution volume. A greater volume of elution buffer can be used, but using less than 40 μL will require extra mixing (to ensure the liquid comes into contact with the beads), and may not be sufficient to elute the entire PCR product.

steps.title: Place the reaction plate onto a...
steps.instruction: Place the reaction plate onto a 96 magnet block for 1 minute to separate beads from the solution.

steps.title: Transfer the eluate to a new plate
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluate to a new plate. Bead carryover into the final plate is usually not a cause for concern. The samples can be stored in the freezer with beads and the beads are inert in downstream enzymatic reactions. If bead carryover must be limited for any reason, 2 μL – 5 μL of eluate can be left behind in the original plate. In addition, a second transfer away from the beads is optional. To do so, place the final plate containing beads and eluate onto the magnet for 1 minute to separate the beads. Transfer the eluate into another clean plate

steps.title: Sample barcoding
steps.instruction: Sample barcoding

steps.title: Save the following Barcoding program on the thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Save the following Barcoding program on the thermal cycler: Choose the preheat lid option and set to 105°C Set the react ion volume to 10 μl 30°C for 2 minutes 80°C for 2 minutes Hold at 10°C

steps.title: Thaw kit components at room temperature,...
steps.instruction: Thaw kit components at room temperature, spin down briefly using a microfuge and mix by pipetting as indicated by the table below: %s %s

steps.title: Prepare the DNA in nuclease-free water, as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare the DNA in nuclease-free water, as follows. Approximately 50 ng of amplicon DNA is required in 9 µl of volume for each sample for barcoding; Dilute your amplicon DNA samples with nuclease-free water to approximately 50 ng Pipette mix the dilutions, and spin down briefly. Add 9 µl of volume for each sample into a 0.2 ml PCR tube or plate.

steps.title: Select a unique barcode for every...
steps.instruction: Select a unique barcode for every sample to be run together on the same flow cell. Up to 96 samples can be barcoded and combined in one experiment. Only use one barcode per sample

steps.title: In 0
steps.instruction: In 0.2 ml thin-walled PCR tubes or plate, mix the following reagents. The Rapid Barcodes can be transferred using a multichannel pipette: %s %s

steps.title: Ensure the components are thoroughly mixed...
steps.instruction: Ensure the components are thoroughly mixed by pipetting and spin down briefly.

steps.title: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler and run the Barcoding program

steps.title: Spin down the tubes or plate...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tubes or plate to collect the liquid at the bottom

steps.title: Pool all the barcoded samples into a clean 1
steps.instruction: Pool all the barcoded samples into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, noting the total volume; %s %s A maximum of 96 samples can be multiplexed

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads (AXP) by vortexing
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads (AXP) by vortexing.

steps.title: To the entire pooled barcoded sample,...
steps.instruction: To the entire pooled barcoded sample, add an equal volume of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) and mix by flicking the tube: %s %s

steps.title: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Prepare at least 3 ml of...
steps.instruction: Prepare at least 3 ml of fresh 80% ethanol in nuclease-free water

steps.title: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet. Keep the tube on the magnet, and pipette off the supernatant.

steps.title: Keep the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Keep the tube on the magnet and wash the beads with 1.5 ml of freshly prepared 80% ethanol without disturbing the pellet. Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step.

steps.title: Briefly spin down and place the...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow to dry for 30 seconds, but do not dry the pellet to the point of cracking

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µl Elution Buffer (EB). Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Pellet the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 1 minute.

steps.title: Remove and retain the eluate which...
steps.instruction: Remove and retain the eluate which contains the DNA library in a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube

steps.title: Quantify 1 µl of eluted sample...
steps.instruction: Quantify 1 µl of eluted sample using a Qubit™ dsDNA Quantification HS Assay Kits Thermo Scientific Catalog #Q32854

steps.title: Transfer 11 µl of the sample into a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 11 µl of the sample into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Transfer a maximum of 800 ng of the DNA library. If necessary, take forward only the necessary volume for 800 ng of DNA library and make up the rest of the volume to 11 µl using Elution Buffer (EB).

steps.title: In a fresh 1
steps.instruction: In a fresh 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, dilute the Rapid Adapter (RA) as follows and pipette mix: %s %s

steps.title: Add 1 µl of the diluted...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of the diluted Rapid Adapter (RA) to the barcoded DNA

steps.title: Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 5 minutes at room temperature. The prepared library is used for loading into the flow cell. Store the library on ice until ready to load.

steps.title: Proceed to prime and load the...
steps.instruction: Proceed to prime and load the MinION or GridION Flow Cell

steps.title: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s
steps.instruction: Record sample preparation details in the table below; %s %s

steps.title: Follow denature and dilute instructions for...
steps.instruction: Follow denature and dilute instructions for the sequencing system to dilute to the final loading concentration

steps.title: Record Reagent Lot Numbers NaOH powder...
steps.instruction: Record Reagent Lot Numbers NaOH powder Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #655104 PhiX Control v3 Illumina, Inc. Catalog #FC-110-3001

steps.title: Prepare Reagents
steps.instruction: Prepare Reagents

steps.title: Prepare 1 ml of fresh 0
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 ml of fresh 0.2 N NaOH Combine the following volumes in a microcentrifuge tube. Laboratory-grade water (800 μl) Stock 1 N NaOH (200 μl) 2. Invert the tube several times to mix Use the fresh dilution within 12 hours

steps.title: Prepare HT1 Remove HT1 from -25°C...
steps.instruction: Prepare HT1 Remove HT1 from -25°C to -15°C storage and thaw at room temperature. Store at 2°C to 8°C until you are ready to dilute denatured libraries.

steps.title: Denature and Dilute, 4 nM Libraries
steps.instruction: Denature and Dilute, 4 nM Libraries

steps.title: Combine the following volumes in a microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Combine the following volumes in a microcentrifuge tube. 4 nM library (5 μl) 0.2 N NaOH (5 μl)

steps.title: Vortex briefly and then centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly and then centrifuge at 280 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Add 990 μl prechilled HT1 to...
steps.instruction: Add 990 μl prechilled HT1 to the tube containing denatured library. The result is 1 ml of a 20 pM denatured library.

steps.title: Dilute Denatured 20 pM Library to...
steps.instruction: Dilute Denatured 20 pM Library to the desired concentration using the following volumes. %s %s

steps.title: Invert to mix and then pulse...
steps.instruction: Invert to mix and then pulse centrifuge This protocol has been optimised at 12 pM.

steps.title: Denature and Dilute PhiX Control
steps.instruction: Denature and Dilute PhiX Control

steps.title: Dilute PhiX to 4 nM by...
steps.instruction: Dilute PhiX to 4 nM by combining the following volumes in a microcentrifuge tube. 10 nM PhiX library (2 μl) 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.5 with 0.1% Tween 20 (3 μl) If not prepared within the last 12 hours, prepare a fresh dilution of 0.2 N NaOH.

steps.title: Denature PhiX Control by combining the...
steps.instruction: Denature PhiX Control by combining the following volumes in a microcentrifuge tube. 4 nM PhiX library (5 μl) 0.2 N NaOH (5 μl)

steps.title: Vortex briefly to mix
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 280 × g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 280 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Dilute Denatured PhiX to Loading Concentration...
steps.instruction: Dilute Denatured PhiX to Loading Concentration The PhiX Control can be denatured and diluted to either 12.5 pM or 20 pM depending on the Miseq reagent version to be used as shown in the table below; %s %s

steps.title: Dilute Denatured PhiX to 20 pM...
steps.instruction: Dilute Denatured PhiX to 20 pM by adding prechilled HT1 to the denatured PhiX library. Denatured PhiX library (10 μl) Prechilled HT1 (990 μl) Invert to mix. You can store the denatured 20 pM PhiX library up to 3 weeks at -15°C to -25°C. After 3 weeks, cluster numbers tend to decrease. Label the Phix with the date

steps.title: Dilute Denatured PhiX to 12
steps.instruction: Dilute Denatured PhiX to 12.5 pM by adding prechilled HT1 to the denatured PhiX library. 20 pM denatured PhiX library (375 μl) Prechilled HT1 (225 μl) Invert to mix. If you are using MiSeq Reagent Kit v3, no further dilution is required.

steps.title: Combine Library and PhiX Control For...
steps.instruction: Combine Library and PhiX Control For most libraries, use a low-concentration PhiX control spike-in of 1% as a sequencing control. For low diversity libraries, increase the PhiX control spike-in to at least 5%.

steps.title: Combine the following volumes of denatured...
steps.instruction: Combine the following volumes of denatured PhiX control and denatured library. %s %s This protocol has been optimised for a 5% Spike-In

steps.title: Set aside on ice until you...
steps.instruction: Set aside on ice until you are ready to load it onto the reagent cartridge for sequencing on a MiSeq System

steps.title: Record Reagent lot numbers MiSeq Reagent Kits Illumina, Inc
steps.instruction: Record Reagent lot numbers MiSeq Reagent Kits Illumina, Inc. Catalog #MS-102-3003, MS-102-2002, MS-103 Our experiments indicated that we can multiplex up to 384 samples on a MiSeq Reagent V3 (600 cycles) flow cell, 192 samples on a MiSeq Reagent V2 (300 cycles) flow cell, 96 samples on a MiSeq Reagent V2 Micro (300 cycles) flow cell, and 48 samples on a MiSeq Reagent V2 Nano (300 cycles) flow cell. These numbers can be adjusted based on laboratory preferences. Our goal is to achieve a coverage of >5000 for each sample

steps.title: Preparation of MiSeq Reagents
steps.instruction: Preparation of MiSeq Reagents

steps.title: Preparation of Sample sheet
steps.instruction: Preparation of Sample sheet

steps.title: Clean Flow cell
steps.instruction: Clean Flow cell

steps.title: Prepare Miseq run
steps.instruction: Prepare Miseq run

steps.title: Retrieve the corresponding sequencing cartridge and...
steps.instruction: Retrieve the corresponding sequencing cartridge and flow cell from the storage

steps.title: Thaw the MiSeq cartridge using one...
steps.instruction: Thaw the MiSeq cartridge using one of the methods below and record the thawing method, start and finish date/times %s %s

steps.title: Open the “Illumina Experiment Manager” icon
steps.instruction: Open the “Illumina Experiment Manager” icon

steps.title: Select ‘ Other ’ and a...
steps.instruction: Select ‘ Other ’ and a second set of options will appear

steps.title: Highlight “MiSeq” and proceed by selecting the “Next” button
steps.instruction: Highlight “MiSeq” and proceed by selecting the “Next” button

steps.title: Select “ Create Sample Sheet ” button
steps.instruction: Select “ Create Sample Sheet ” button

steps.title: On the “Workflow Parameters” section, complete...
steps.instruction: On the “Workflow Parameters” section, complete the “FASTQ Only Run Settings” as follows: Reagent Cartridge Barcode: Enter the MiSeq run number. Library Prep Workflow: Illumina DNA Prep Index Adapters: Specify the Illumina UD Indexes Index reads: 2 Experiment Names: MiSeq run number . Investigator Name: Enter the initials of the operator . Description: Leave blank . Date: Select date of sequencing . Read-type: Paired-end . Cycles Read 1: 151 Cycles Read 2: 151

steps.title: Select ‘ FASTQ Only ’ and click ‘Next’
steps.instruction: Select ‘ FASTQ Only ’ and click ‘Next’

steps.title: Select the “Next” button
steps.instruction: Select the “Next” button

steps.title: Ensure the “FASTQ Only Workflow Specific”...
steps.instruction: Ensure the “FASTQ Only Workflow Specific” settings are as follows: Custom Primer for Read 1: Box unchecked Custom Primer for Index: Box unchecked Custom Primer for Read 2: Box unchecked Reverse Complement: Box unchecked Use Adapter Trimming: Box checked Use Adapter Trimming Read 2: Box checked

steps.title: Select “Add Blank Row” to add...
steps.instruction: Select “Add Blank Row” to add rows and manually enter sample information. To remove rows, select at least one field, and then select “Remove Selected Rows”.

steps.title: Enter the following information in the fields listed below
steps.instruction: Enter the following information in the fields listed below: Sample ID: Name of sample . Index Well: Indices assigned to the samples

steps.title: Click ‘Finish’ to complete the worksheet
steps.instruction: Click ‘Finish’ to complete the worksheet. Save the sample sheet to a USB stick or on a computer, using the Sequencing Run ID as the file name The sample can as well be done or finalized on a separate computer

steps.title: Ensure the “Sample Sheet Status” in...
steps.instruction: Ensure the “Sample Sheet Status” in the bottom left corner is deemed “valid” If the Sample Sheet Status is deemed “invalid”, the reason will be displayed in the “Reason” field in the bottom left corner. Any spaces or symbols in the sample ID may make the sheet invalid, as will conflicting indexes

steps.title: Using plastic forceps, grip the flow...
steps.instruction: Using plastic forceps, grip the flow cell by the base of the plastic cartridge and remove it from the flow cell container

steps.title: Dry excess alcohol with a lint-free lens cleaning tissue
steps.instruction: Dry excess alcohol with a lint-free lens cleaning tissue

steps.title: Clean the flow cell glass with an alcohol wipe
steps.instruction: Clean the flow cell glass with an alcohol wipe. Make sure that the glass is free of streaks, fingerprints, and lint or tissue fibers. Do not use the alcohol wipe on the flow cell port gasket.

steps.title: With care, wipe around the black...
steps.instruction: With care, wipe around the black flow cell port gasket, thoroughly dry the flow cell and cartridge with a lint free lens cleaning tissue. Gently pat dry in the area of the gasket and adjacent glass

steps.title: Lightly rinse the flow cell with...
steps.instruction: Lightly rinse the flow cell with laboratory-grade water until both the glass and plastic cartridge are thoroughly rinsed of excess salts

steps.title: If the gasket appears to be...
steps.instruction: If the gasket appears to be dislodged, gently press it back into place until it sits securely around the flow cell ports

steps.title: Make sure that the flow cell...
steps.instruction: Make sure that the flow cell ports are free of obstructions and that the gasket is well-seated around the flow cell ports

steps.title: Open the Miseq Control Software (MCS)...
steps.instruction: Open the Miseq Control Software (MCS) to the ‘Welcome Screen’ and select the ‘Sequence’ button

steps.title: If you opt for “BaseSpace”
steps.instruction: If you opt for “BaseSpace”: Log on to base space, otherwise proceed to load flow cell

steps.title: Select the tick box for ‘Use...
steps.instruction: Select the tick box for ‘Use BaseSpace for storage and analysis’

steps.title: Click ‘Next’
steps.instruction: Click ‘Next’. The ‘Load Flow Cell’ screen will be visible

steps.title: Open the Flow Cell compartment door
steps.instruction: Open the Flow Cell compartment door

steps.title: Press the white button to open...
steps.instruction: Press the white button to open the Flow Cell latch and push the latch fully open

steps.title: Throw the used cell from the...
steps.instruction: Throw the used cell from the previous run into a sharps bin and replace with the cleaned flow cell. Align the cell with the two prongs on the flow cell platform

steps.title: Hold the flow cell in place...
steps.instruction: Hold the flow cell in place while slowly closing the latch to prevent dislodging of the flow cell

steps.title: The MiSeq software will read and...
steps.instruction: The MiSeq software will read and record the integrated radio-frequency identification (RFID) tag on the cell before the user is able to proceed to the next stage

steps.title: Once this is done, click ‘Next’...
steps.instruction: Once this is done, click ‘Next’ button and close compartment door

steps.title: The ‘Load Reagents’ screen is now visible
steps.instruction: The ‘Load Reagents’ screen is now visible. Open thereagents compartment door and move the sipper lever into the ‘up’ position.

steps.title: Remove the waste container and empty into a laboratory sink
steps.instruction: Remove the waste container and empty into a laboratory sink. Replace back into the waste container location

steps.title: Remove wash bottle and replace with PR2 buffer
steps.instruction: Remove wash bottle and replace with PR2 buffer

steps.title: Lower the sipper lever and click “next”
steps.instruction: Lower the sipper lever and click “next”

steps.title: Wait for the reagent chiller to...
steps.instruction: Wait for the reagent chiller to release the wash cartridge before opening.

steps.title: Remove wash cartridge and discard the used wash buffer
steps.instruction: Remove wash cartridge and discard the used wash buffer.

steps.title: Invert the reagent cartridge a minimum...
steps.instruction: Invert the reagent cartridge a minimum ten times to fully mix the reagents. Visually inspect the reagent at position 1 to make sure that it is fully thawed and free of precipitates

steps.title: Gently tap the cartridge on the...
steps.instruction: Gently tap the cartridge on the bench to reduce air bubbles in the reagents The MiSeq sipper tubes go to the bottom of each reservoir to aspirate the reagents,so the reservoirs must be free of air bubbles.

steps.title: Using a clean 1ml pipette tip,...
steps.instruction: Using a clean 1ml pipette tip, pierce the foil seal over the reservoir labelled ‘Load Samples’ ( highlighted in orange ). Other reagent positions do not need to be pierced as they are done automatically during the sequencing run

steps.title: Vortex and centrifuge denatured library and...
steps.instruction: Vortex and centrifuge denatured library and transfer the full content (~600μl) of the tube into the ‘Load Samples’ reservoir

steps.title: Insert the reagent cartridge and close...
steps.instruction: Insert the reagent cartridge and close the chiller and compartment door. The MiSeq software will read and record the integrated RFID tag on the Reagent Cartridge before the user is able to proceed to the next stage.

steps.title: Press “Change Sample Sheet” and select...
steps.instruction: Press “Change Sample Sheet” and select the sample sheet prepared earlier

steps.title: A review of the run parameters...
steps.instruction: A review of the run parameters will be visible for verification. Select the sample sheet created. Click ‘Next’

steps.title: The system performs a pre-run check...
steps.instruction: The system performs a pre-run check of all components, disk space and network connections. This can take up to 10 minutes

steps.title: Once all pre-run checks have been successful, click ‘Start Run’
steps.instruction: Once all pre-run checks have been successful, click ‘Start Run’

steps.title: Confirm the run has started before...
steps.instruction: Confirm the run has started before leaving Once started, do not open the compartment doors or touch the instrument monitor except to pause/stop the run. It is wise to detach a mouse and keyboard from the machine.

steps.title: When sequencing has been completed; Record...
steps.instruction: When sequencing has been completed; Record the following on the screen in the table below; %s %s

steps.title: Select the ‘Next’ button to continue...
steps.instruction: Select the ‘Next’ button to continue with a MiSeq Post-run wash.

steps.title: In-House Pipeline HIV-DRIVES is a web...
steps.instruction: In-House Pipeline HIV-DRIVES is a web resource for the in-house pipeline designed for HIV drug resistance profiling, variant evaluation, and surveillance. It provides an easy-to-read PDF report alongside other output files. HIV-DRIVES supports Illumina data in paired and single-ended Fastq formats, including Oxford Nanopore Technology, as well as Fasta and multiple Fasta files from Sanger data in ab1.seq format. It separates patient reads from viral reads before proceeding with downstream processes. HIV-DRIVES is supported by the Stanford University HIV Drug Resistance Database ( HIVdb Program: Mutations Analysis - Stanford Coronavirus Antiviral & Resistance Database (CoVDB) ), Sierra-local ( https://github.com/PoonLab/sierra-local ), and Quasitools ( https://phac-nml.github.io/quasitools/ ), specifically Hydra ( Home - HyDRA Web ). The Stanford HIVdb algorithm s ai widely used method for predicting HIV-1 drug resistance phenotypes based on genetic sequences. Sierra-local, a Python 3 implementation of the Stanford HIVdb Sierra web service, allows laboratories to generate drug resistance predictions from HIV-1 sequence data without transmitting patient data over the network, ensuring data control and security. Quasitools is a collection of tools for analyzing viral quasispecies data. HyDRA is a bioinformatics pipeline that analyzes next-generation sequencing data for HIV-1 drug resistance mutations, using an annotated HXB2 sequence for reference mapping by Bowtie2, stringent data quality assurance, and variant calling. It identifies HIV drug resistance mutations based on the Stanford HIV Drug Resistance Database and the 2009 WHO list for Surveillance of Transmitted HIVDR. All mutations found in the pol gene (protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase) are reported according to the Stanford Surveillance Drug Resistance Mutation list.

steps.title: Priming and loading the MinION and GridION Flow Cell
steps.instruction: Priming and loading the MinION and GridION Flow Cell

steps.title: On-line Pipeline The Stanford University HIV...
steps.instruction: On-line Pipeline The Stanford University HIV Drug Resistance Database is a widely used tool for predicting HIV-1 drug resistance phenotypes. It has evolved to accommodate both Sanger and next generation sequencing (NGS) datasets. For NGS, the program accepts user-submitted protease, RT, and integrase codon frequency tables (CodFreq files) or AAVF files generated by the HYDRA pipeline. Additionally, it features a portal for converting Fastq files to CodFreq files, providing a comprehensive, online-based analysis platform for both Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technology datasets. Link > ( HIVdb Program: Sequence Reads (NGS) Analysis - Stanford Coronavirus Antiviral & Resistance Database (CoVDB) )

steps.title: In-House Pipeline HIV-DRIVES is a web...
steps.instruction: In-House Pipeline HIV-DRIVES is a web resource for the in-house pipeline designed for HIV drug resistance profiling, variant evaluation, and surveillance. It provides an easy-to-read PDF report alongside other output files. HIV-DRIVES supports Illumina data in paired and single-ended Fastq formats, including Oxford Nanopore Technology, as well as Fasta and multiple Fasta files from Sanger data in ab1.seq format. It separates patient reads from viral reads before proceeding with downstream processes. HIV-DRIVES is supported by the Stanford University HIV Drug Resistance Database ( HIVdb Program: Mutations Analysis - Stanford Coronavirus Antiviral & Resistance Database (CoVDB) ), Sierra-local ( https://github.com/PoonLab/sierra-local ), and Quasitools ( https://phac-nml.github.io/quasitools/ ), specifically Hydra ( Home - HyDRA Web ). The Stanford HIVdb algorithm s ai widely used method for predicting HIV-1 drug resistance phenotypes based on genetic sequences. Sierra-local, a Python 3 implementation of the Stanford HIVdb Sierra web service, allows laboratories to generate drug resistance predictions from HIV-1 sequence data without transmitting patient data over the network, ensuring data control and security. Quasitools is a collection of tools for analyzing viral quasispecies data. HyDRA is a bioinformatics pipeline that analyzes next-generation sequencing data for HIV-1 drug resistance mutations, using an annotated HXB2 sequence for reference mapping by Bowtie2, stringent data quality assurance, and variant calling. It identifies HIV drug resistance mutations based on the Stanford HIV Drug Resistance Database and the 2009 WHO list for Surveillance of Transmitted HIVDR. All mutations found in the pol gene (protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase) are reported according to the Stanford Surveillance Drug Resistance Mutation list.

steps.title: On-line Pipeline The Stanford University HIV...
steps.instruction: On-line Pipeline The Stanford University HIV Drug Resistance Database is a widely used tool for predicting HIV-1 drug resistance phenotypes. It has evolved to accommodate both Sanger and next generation sequencing (NGS) datasets. For NGS, the program accepts user-submitted protease, RT, and integrase codon frequency tables (CodFreq files) or AAVF files generated by the HYDRA pipeline. Additionally, it features a portal for converting Fastq files to CodFreq files, providing a comprehensive, online-based analysis platform for both Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technology datasets. Link > ( HIVdb Program: Sequence Reads (NGS) Analysis - Stanford Coronavirus Antiviral & Resistance Database (CoVDB) )

steps.title: Record Reagent Lot Number
steps.instruction: Record Reagent Lot Number: QIAamp Viral RNA Mini Kit Qiagen Catalog #52906

steps.title: Dissolve the carrier RNA thoroughly, divide...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the carrier RNA thoroughly, divide it into conveniently sized aliquots, and store it at -30 °C to -15 °C . Do not freeze-thaw the aliquots of carrier RNA more than 3 times

steps.title: Record Reagent lot numbers; Flow Cell (R10
steps.instruction: Record Reagent lot numbers; Flow Cell (R10.4.1) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #FLO-MIN114 UltraPure™ BSA (50 mg/mL) Thermo Scientific Catalog #AM2616

steps.title: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library Beads (LIB) or Library Solution (LIS, if using), Flow Cell Tether (FCT) and Flow Cell Flush (FCF) at room temperature before mixing by vortexing. Then spin down and store on ice.

steps.title: Setting up a Sequencing Run on a MinKNOW
steps.instruction: Setting up a Sequencing Run on a MinKNOW

steps.title: To prepare the flow cell priming...
steps.instruction: To prepare the flow cell priming mix with BSA, combine Flow Cell Flush (FCF) and Flow Cell Tether (FCT), as directed below. Mix by pipetting at room temperature. %s %s

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the contents of the...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the contents of the Library Beads (LIB) by pipetting.

steps.title: Load 800 µl of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 800 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles. Wait for five minutes.

steps.title: After opening the priming port, check...
steps.instruction: After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Draw back a small volume to remove any bubbles: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl Insert the tip into the priming port Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µl, to draw back 20-30 µl, or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip Visually check that there is continuous buffer from the priming port across the sensor array.

steps.title: Slide the flow cell priming port...
steps.instruction: Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port.

steps.title: Open the MinION or GridION device...
steps.instruction: Open the MinION or GridION device lid and slide the flow cell under the clip. Press down firmly on the flow cell to ensure correct thermal and electrical contact. Complete a flow cell check to assess the number of pores available before loading the library.

steps.title: Mix the prepared library gently by...
steps.instruction: Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just prior to loading.

steps.title: Complete the flow cell priming
steps.instruction: Complete the flow cell priming: Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible. Load 200 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port ( not the SpotON sample port), avoiding the introduction of air bubbles.

steps.title: Place the light shield onto the flow cell, as follows
steps.instruction: Place the light shield onto the flow cell, as follows: Carefully place the leading edge of the light shield against the clip. Note: Do not force the light shield underneath the clip. Gently lower the light shield onto the flow cell. The light shield should sit around the SpotON cover, covering the entire top section of the flow cell.

steps.title: Gently replace the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port and close the priming port.

steps.title: Add 75 μl of the prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 75 μl of the prepared library to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion. Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next.

steps.title: Close the device lid and set...
steps.instruction: Close the device lid and set up a sequencing run on MinKNOW.

steps.title: Navigate to the start page and click Start sequencing
steps.instruction: Navigate to the start page and click Start sequencing.

steps.title: Set up the output format and filtering as follows
steps.instruction: Set up the output format and filtering as follows: Select either .POD5 or .FAST5 (legacy) as the output format. Ensure .FASTQ is selected for basecalled reads. Ensure filtering is ON and read splitting is enabled. Other parameters can be kept to their default settings. Click Continue to final review to continue.

steps.title: Set up basecalling and barcoding using...
steps.instruction: Set up basecalling and barcoding using the following parameters: Toggle basecalling to ON. Next to "Models", click Edit options and choose High accuracy basecaller (HAC) from the drop-down menu. Toggle barcoding to ON. Keep all other options at their default settings. Click Continue to output and continue.

steps.title: Change the run limit to 12...
steps.instruction: Change the run limit to 12 hours by clicking "Options" and changing the run limit value to 12. The other run settings can be left at the defaults.

steps.title: Select the Rapid Barcoding Kit 24 V14 (SQK-RBK114
steps.instruction: Select the Rapid Barcoding Kit 24 V14 (SQK-RBK114.24) or Rapid Barcoding Kit 96 V14 (SQK-RBK114.96).

steps.title: Fill in your experiment details, such...
steps.instruction: Fill in your experiment details, such as name and flow cell position and sample ID.

steps.title: Proceed to Data analysis after sequencing
steps.instruction: Proceed to Data analysis after sequencing

steps.title: Click Start on the Review page...
steps.instruction: Click Start on the Review page to start the sequencing run.

steps.title: Routine patient care results are presented...
steps.instruction: Routine patient care results are presented in a standard report format. This format can be customized based on laboratory preferences or clinical team recommendations for optimal interpretation. The result format is primarily generated as a JSON file for seamless integration between the in-house laboratory information management system and the national HIV drug resistance database. This database enables healthcare facilities to remotely access patient results and efficiently manage their clients.

steps.title: The results for routine patient care...
steps.instruction: The results for routine patient care are presented as the report format. This could be adjusted based on laboratory preference or with guidance from the clinical team for quick interpretation; The result format is primarily generated as a JSON file for seamless integration between the in-house laboratory information management system and the national HIV drug resistance database. This database enables healthcare facilities to remotely access patient results and efficiently manage their clients.
